ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-23-2021 Continued To Mar-23-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Inheritance Rights Of Leslie Isaac
Off Calendar Feb-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Boessenecker appeared by zoom for Petitioner.
Michael Freedman appeared by zoom for Annette Daniele (Administrator).

The Court noted that at the last hearing it requested more information and notice to Barry Mitchell, and that it received additional information and a proof of service on Mr. Mitchell. Mr. Boessenecker stated that he has nothing to add. Mr. Freedman stated that he represents the Administrator, that they are remaining neutral, that Annette provided a detailed declaration and that she supports the petition. The Court noted that one of Mr. Boessenecker's pleadings shows W.C. Cox & Co. as his Client. Mr. Boessenecker clarified that his Client is Leslie Isaac and stated that he got the caption fouled up and that it was a clerical error. The Petition to Determine Inheritance Rights of Leslie Isaac is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate

Off Calendar Dec-24-2020 Continued To Jan-26-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Inheritance Rights Of Leslie Isaac
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Continued To Dec-29-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Dec-02-2020 Continued To Jan-13-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Inheritance Rights Of Leslie Isaac
Off Calendar Nov-03-2020 Continued To Dec-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker, Esq., counsel for Leslie Isaac (appearing by video conference, audio only).

At the Court's query, Mr. Boessenecker reports his understanding that Mr. Freeman need not appear today as the matter now before the Court does not directly affect his client.
The Court notes the various efforts to provide Mr. Mitchell with notice of the proceedings but finds that the efforts made thus far do not appear to meet the requirements of Probate Code Sec. 6452 and in particular, subsection (c)(3). The Court further finds that the evidence submitted thus far of abandonment of the minor by Mr. Mitchell - such as it is - is at best persuasive but is not supported by any declaration reflecting personal knowledge of any declarant. The Court further notes that the Petition was not verified by the Petitioner but rather by counsel which the Court finds insufficient for evidentiary purposes. In short, the Court will require additional evidence to support sufficient notice to Mr. Mitchell and that he has, in fact, abandoned the minor.

The Court continues the matter to December 15, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Deputy: K. Kabasares; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

Petition To Determine Inheritance Rights Of Leslie Isaac
Off Calendar Oct-06-2020 Continued To Nov-03-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Randall Boessenecker, Esq., counsel for Leslie Issac, Petitioner (appearing by video conference).

The Court initially addresses concern with regard to the ostensibly ersatz service to one Barry Mitchell, noting that service was to a P.O. Box only. Mr. Boessenecker responds, advising the Court that the P.O. Box is the only known address for Mr. Mitchell but that he has orally acknowledged receipt of the Petition. The Court notes that oral acknowledgement notwithstanding, some evidence of due diligence in attempting to otherwise serve Mr. Mitchell or his written acknowledgement of receipt would be in order.

Upon inquiry from the Court, Mr. Boessenecker confirms that Petitioner Isaac resides in the State of Washington and signed the verification of the Petition in her capacity as an heir and has no fiduciary capacity. The Court also addresses the issue of child support to which Mr. Boessenecker responds. The Court directs Mr. Boessenecker to report the same in supplemental pleadings.

The Court continues the matter to November 3, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Johnson. (D204)
